prompt: Do not use <think> tags.
Do not reason before generating.
Your response must begin with <nn> immediately.
Your first output character must be '<'.
Do not write any text before <nn>.
Do not write any text after </tr>.
Output only these three XML blocks in order: <nn>...</nn><hp>...</hp><tr>...</tr>.
You are generating a LEMUR-compatible neural network for dataset='{dataset}', task='{task}', metric='{metric}', epoch={epoch}.
Target accuracy above {acc_3}.
Use Reference A as the structural base. Integrate at most two improvements from References B and C combined.
Use at most two backbone models total — never three.

Only use these backbone models: 'resnet50', 'densenet169', 'mobilenet_v3_large', 'efficientnet_b0', 'efficientnet_b3'. Never use efficientnet_b4, resnext50_32x4d, or swin_t — they are too large for this GPU.
Never pass keyword arguments to backbone forward calls — use self.backbone_a(x) not self.backbone_a(x, kwarg=value).
supported_hyperparameters() must return exactly: 'batch', 'dropout', 'epoch', 'lr', 'momentum', 'transform'.
Every key in supported_hyperparameters() must appear at least twice in the code by exact name.
The <hp> block must contain a valid JSON dict — example: {{"batch": 32, "dropout": 0.2, "epoch": 1, "lr": 0.01, "momentum": 0.9, "transform": "norm_256_flip"}}.
Never generate a list in <hp> — only a JSON dict.
The 'transform' value in <hp> must be exactly 'norm_256_flip'.
Net.__init__ signature must be: def __init__(self, in_shape, out_shape, prm, device).
self.features() must always receive the raw input tensor x — never a concatenation of backbone outputs.
Never create a variable that combines backbone outputs and pass it to self.features().
Wrong: mid = torch.cat([f_a, f_b], dim=1); self.features(mid)
Right: x_f = adaptive_pool_flatten(self.features(x))
Reference A (structural anchor, accuracy={acc_1}):
<hp>{hp_1}</hp>
<tr>{tr_1}</tr>
<nn>{nn_1}</nn>
Reference B (accuracy={acc_2}):
<hp>{hp_2}</hp>
<tr>{tr_2}</tr>
<nn>{nn_2}</nn>

<hp>{hp_3}</hp>
<tr>{tr_3}</tr>
<nn>{nn_3}</nn>
